blueprint.title: Day-of Operations AI: LTL Exception Management, Dock Orchestration, and Driver Check-In Monitoring

personas.name: Load Planning Supervisor at a Regional LTL Carrier
personas.description: Oversees daily manifest execution across 12-15 linehaul lanes, responsible for re-routing decisions when weather or road closures disrupt planned routes mid-shift.
goals: Minimize service failures caused by unplanned route deviations
Reduce manual phone calls to drivers and terminal managers during exceptions
Maintain on-time delivery percentage above 96% even on disrupted days
pain_points: Weather and closure alerts arrive through disparate sources with no unified view
Re-routing decisions currently require manual cross-referencing of load boards, driver HOS logs, and customer SLAs
Downstream dock teams are rarely notified of route changes in time to adjust staffing

personas.name: Dock Operations Manager at a Regional LTL Break-Bulk Terminal
personas.description: Manages inbound and outbound trailer flow across 28 dock doors, coordinating lumpers, forklift operators, and yard spotters when trailers arrive outside their scheduled windows.
goals: Keep freight moving through the dock without creating unload backlogs
Dynamically reassign dock doors and labor when trailers arrive late or early
Reduce trailer dwell time below 45 minutes per inbound unit
pain_points: Off-schedule trailer arrivals cause door conflicts and idle labor
No real-time visibility into which trailers are 30-60 minutes out versus severely delayed
Manual whiteboard-based door assignments cannot keep pace with rapid schedule changes

personas.name: Fleet Safety & Compliance Coordinator at a Regional LTL Carrier
personas.description: Monitors driver check-in compliance, missed stop reporting, and hours-of-service adherence across a fleet of 85 local and linehaul drivers.
goals: Detect missed stops within minutes rather than hours to enable same-day recovery
Ensure drivers complete mandatory check-ins at defined intervals without dispatcher intervention
Escalate safety-relevant deviations to dispatch before they become service failures
pain_points: Missed check-ins are often discovered only when a customer calls to report a missed delivery
Dispatchers are overwhelmed and cannot proactively monitor all active drivers simultaneously
No automated differentiation between a driver running late versus a driver who has genuinely missed a stop

scenarios.title: Winter Storm Route Exception and Dock Resequencing
scenarios.description: An unexpected ice storm closes I-78 westbound at 06:15, affecting three active linehaul runs and causing four trailers to arrive at the break-bulk terminal between 90 and 150 minutes behind schedule. The Load Planning Supervisor and Dock Operations Manager must collaborate with AI agents to re-route drivers, notify consignees, and resequence dock door assignments before the morning sort window closes.
steps: Route Exception Monitor Agent detects the I-78 closure via ATMS feed at 06:17 and cross-references all active manifests to identify three affected drivers and 47 impacted shipments.

Dynamic Re-Route Agent calculates alternate paths for each affected driver using current HOS balances, customer delivery windows, and real-time traffic data, then surfaces ranked re-route options to the Load Planning Supervisor in the dispatch console.

Load Planning Supervisor approves the recommended re-routes with one-click confirmation; agents push updated turn-by-turn instructions to driver ELD units and trigger automated customer delay notifications for affected consignees.

Dock Orchestration Agent receives revised ETA projections for the four delayed trailers and generates an updated door assignment plan, reallocating two doors from outbound staging to inbound unload and adjusting lumper shift start times by 75 minutes.

Dock Operations Manager reviews the AI-generated door plan on the terminal dashboard, accepts the resequencing, and the agent broadcasts updated assignments to yard spotters via the yard management system.

scenarios.title: Missed Stop Detection and Same-Day Recovery Dispatch
scenarios.description: A local P&D driver on a 14-stop city route fails to scan at stop 9 and does not submit the required 11:00 check-in. The Fleet Safety & Compliance Coordinator must identify the deviation, determine whether it is a missed stop or a delay, and coordinate a recovery pickup before the customer's receiving dock closes at 15:00.
steps: Driver Check-In Monitor Agent detects the absence of the 11:00 check-in at 11:04 and immediately queries the ELD for the driver's last known GPS position, engine status, and stop scan history.

The agent determines the driver's GPS position has not moved for 22 minutes near stop 8 and that stop 9 scan is absent, classifying the event as a probable missed stop rather than a traffic delay, and escalates an alert to the Fleet Safety & Compliance Coordinator.

Fleet Safety & Compliance Coordinator reviews the AI-generated incident summary including stop 9 consignee details, freight weight, and the 15:00 receiving cutoff, then authorizes the agent to attempt automated driver contact via in-cab messaging.

Driver responds confirming stop 9 was skipped due to a locked facility; Recovery Dispatch Agent identifies the nearest available driver with remaining HOS capacity and a compatible route segment, and proposes a recovery dispatch plan to the Load Planning Supervisor.

Load Planning Supervisor approves the recovery assignment; Recovery Dispatch Agent updates both drivers' manifests, notifies the stop 9 consignee of the revised delivery ETA, and logs the exception in the TMS for service failure reporting.

agents.name: Route Exception Monitor
agents.role: Real-time detection of road network disruptions affecting active manifests
responsibilities: Continuously ingest traffic incident feeds, ATMS closure data, and weather alert APIs
Cross-reference active driver manifests against affected road segments in real time
Classify exception severity based on number of impacted shipments, customer SLA risk, and estimated delay duration
Trigger downstream re-route and notification workflows within defined latency thresholds
inputs: State DOT ATMS road closure feeds
National Weather Service precipitation and road condition alerts
Active manifest and driver assignment data from TMS
Real-time traffic speed data from HERE or Google Maps Platform
outputs: Structured exception event records with affected driver IDs, shipment counts, and estimated delay windows
Severity-ranked alert payloads routed to dispatch console and downstream agents
Audit log entries for each detected exception

agents.name: Dynamic Re-Route Planner
agents.role: Generates and ranks alternate routing options for drivers affected by exceptions
responsibilities: Calculate feasible alternate routes for each affected driver using current HOS balance, vehicle weight class, and road restrictions
Score route options against customer delivery window compliance, fuel cost delta, and driver familiarity
Push approved route updates to ELD and driver mobile units
Trigger automated consignee delay notifications for shipments that cannot meet original windows
inputs: Exception event records from Route Exception Monitor Agent
Driver HOS logs from ELD integration
Customer SLA and delivery window data from TMS
Mapping and routing engine API with truck-specific restrictions
outputs: Ranked re-route option sets presented in dispatch console for supervisor approval
Approved turn-by-turn route updates transmitted to driver ELD units
Automated delay notification messages to consignees via email and EDI 214
Revised ETA projections forwarded to Dock Orchestration Agent

agents.name: Dock Orchestration Coordinator
agents.role: Dynamically resequences dock door assignments and labor allocation based on revised trailer ETAs
responsibilities: Maintain a live model of dock door availability, trailer queue, and labor headcount
Recalculate door assignments when trailer ETAs shift by more than a configurable threshold
Adjust lumper and forklift operator schedules to match revised unload sequences
Broadcast updated assignments to yard management system and terminal staff devices
inputs: Revised trailer ETA projections from Dynamic Re-Route Planner Agent
Current dock door status and trailer queue from yard management system
Labor schedule and headcount data from workforce management system
Outbound sort window deadlines from linehaul dispatch plan
outputs: Updated dock door assignment plan displayed on terminal operations dashboard
Labor reallocation instructions pushed to shift supervisor mobile app
Door assignment updates written to yard management system
Dwell time projections and backlog risk alerts for Dock Operations Manager

agents.name: Driver Check-In Monitor
agents.role: Detects missed check-ins and classifies stop compliance deviations across the active driver fleet
responsibilities: Monitor scheduled check-in windows for all active drivers and flag non-compliance within minutes of missed deadlines
Query ELD telemetry to distinguish between traffic delay, vehicle breakdown, and missed stop scenarios
Classify deviation type and severity using stop scan history, GPS dwell patterns, and route progress
Escalate confirmed missed stop events to Fleet Safety & Compliance Coordinator with full context
inputs: Driver check-in schedule from TMS dispatch plan
Real-time ELD telemetry including GPS position, engine status, and stop scan events
Planned stop sequence and geofence boundaries for each active route
Historical driver check-in compliance data for anomaly baseline
outputs: Missed check-in alerts with classification and supporting telemetry evidence
Incident summary reports delivered to Fleet Safety & Compliance Coordinator console
Automated in-cab driver contact messages requesting status confirmation
Escalation records logged in TMS for compliance reporting

agents.name: Recovery Dispatch Optimizer
agents.role: Identifies and assigns the optimal available driver to execute same-day missed stop recoveries
responsibilities: Search active and returning driver pool for candidates with sufficient HOS, geographic proximity, and compatible freight capability
Score recovery candidates against delivery window feasibility, incremental mileage cost, and customer priority tier
Generate updated manifests for both the original driver and the recovery driver
Notify consignees of revised delivery ETAs and coordinate dock appointment adjustments where required
inputs: Missed stop incident records from Driver Check-In Monitor Agent
Active driver HOS balances and current GPS positions from ELD integration
Consignee receiving hours and dock appointment data from TMS
Freight characteristics including weight, dimensions, and handling requirements
outputs: Ranked recovery driver assignment recommendations presented to Load Planning Supervisor
Updated manifest files transmitted to assigned recovery driver ELD unit
Revised ETA notifications sent to affected consignees
Exception and recovery event records written to TMS for KPI tracking

agents.name: Operations Intelligence Summarizer
agents.role: Aggregates cross-agent event data into shift-level operational summaries and trend alerts for terminal leadership
responsibilities: Compile exception events, re-routes, dock resequencing actions, and missed stop recoveries into a unified shift log
Calculate real-time impact metrics including service failure risk count, average delay duration, and dock dwell variance
Surface pattern alerts when exception frequency exceeds historical norms for a given lane, driver, or time window
Deliver end-of-shift summary reports to terminal manager and regional operations director
inputs: Event and output records from all other agents in the pipeline
TMS historical performance baselines for lanes, drivers, and terminals
Customer SLA compliance thresholds from contract management system
outputs: Live shift operations dashboard with exception counts, recovery status, and dock throughput metrics
Pattern deviation alerts pushed to terminal manager when thresholds are breached
End-of-shift PDF and structured data summary reports distributed to regional leadership
Trend data written to data warehouse for weekly and monthly performance analysis
